text.title: A/B Test Worked Examples
text.summary: Three worked examples — one clear win, one marginal win, one failure — demonstrating hypothesis format, result interpretation, and decision logic.

example.note: Hypothesis: IF we change CTA from "Sign Up" to "Start Free Trial", THEN conversion will increase 8%, BECAUSE it emphasizes the free offer. Baseline 4.5%, MDE 8% relative (~0.36pp absolute), n=50k/variant, 3 weeks. Result: A=4.50% (n=52340), B=4.90% (n=51890), diff=+0.40pp (+8.9% relative), p=0.003 (99.7% confidence). Decision: ship B.

Hypothesis: IF we add "Most Popular" badge to Pro plan, THEN Pro selection will increase 15%, BECAUSE social proof influences choice. Result: A=15.0% (n=8450), B=17.0% (n=8380), diff=+2.0pp (+13.3%), p=0.048 (95.2% confidence). Decision: ship B — positive direction with sufficient confidence, even though below 15% MDE target.

Hypothesis: IF we add testimonials above the fold, THEN conversions will increase 10%, BECAUSE social proof builds trust. Result: A=5.00% (n=12000), B=4.79% (n=12200), diff=-0.21pp (-4.2%), p=0.32 (68% confidence). Decision: keep A (no significance, and directionally negative). Learning: testimonials may have distracted from the CTA; test placement below the fold instead.

rule.statement: When a test fails directionally, document the specific learning, not just "hypothesis was wrong" — failed tests are the most informative entries in the experiment log.
rule.rationale: The experiment log is the cumulative knowledge base for the growth function; vague failure entries cannot inform future test design.

antipattern.description: Peeking at interim results and stopping early when a winner appears.
antipattern.reason: Inflates false positives 5–10x; the test is only valid at the pre-committed sample size and end date.

antipattern.description: Testing multiple simultaneous changes in one variant.
antipattern.reason: Cannot isolate which change caused the effect; the test produces an uninterpretable compound result.

antipattern.description: Ignoring guardrail metrics after a primary win.
antipattern.reason: Pyrrhic victories — e.g. a signup-rate win that increases D7 churn — destroy more value than they create.
